pageDescription: IM channel configuration (Feishu, WeCom, WeChat, DingTalk, Telegram, WhatsApp)
cardDesc: Configure IM bot integrations to allow AI Agents to receive and send messages on Feishu, WeCom, and other platforms.
restartHint: Manually stop and re-establish the long connection

empty.title: No Channels
empty.description: No registered IM channel plugins detected.
empty.selectChannelHint: Select a channel from the left to configure it.

feishu.enableDesc: When enabled, the bot will connect to Feishu and start receiving and replying to messages.
feishu.credentialsDesc: Fill in after creating a custom app in the Feishu Open Platform.
feishu.appIdHint: App ID of the Feishu custom app (starts with cli_)
feishu.appSecretHint: App Secret of the Feishu custom app
feishu.connectionModeHint: WebSocket long-connection (recommended, no public IP required) or Webhook callback (requires public URL)
feishu.domainHint: Choose Feishu for mainland China, Lark for overseas
feishu.encryptKeyHint: Used for message encryption in Webhook mode (optional)
feishu.verificationTokenHint: Used to verify request origin in Webhook mode (optional)
feishu.behaviorDesc: Configure message routing and trigger conditions.
feishu.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
feishu.groupTriggerHint: When to trigger Agent response in group chats
feishu.allowFromHint: Only allow these user IDs to trigger Agent (leave empty to allow everyone)
feishu.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter user ID and press Enter to add
feishu.reactionNotificationsHint: Whether to trigger callback on emoji reactions
feishu.mentionContextMessagesHint: Number of recent non-mention messages to include as context when @mentioned (0 to disable)
feishu.advancedDesc: Streaming output, debounce, deduplication, and other advanced parameters.
feishu.streamingHint: When enabled, replies are rendered as streaming cards (requires cardkit:card:write permission)
feishu.streamingCoalesceMsHint: Throttle window for streaming card appends (milliseconds)
feishu.inboundDebounceMsHint: Wait time for merging consecutive messages, 0 to disable (default 800ms)
feishu.dedupTtlSecondsHint: Retention time for message deduplication records (default 86400s = 24h)
feishu.multiAccountDesc: Configure multiple Feishu app accounts, each with independent credentials and connection settings.
feishu.multiAccountHint: Add accounts here for multi-account setup, or fill in single account credentials above.

wecom.enableDesc: When enabled, the bot will connect to WeCom via WebSocket and start receiving and replying to messages.
wecom.credentialsDesc: Fill in after creating a bot application in the WeCom admin console.
wecom.botIdHint: Bot ID of the WeCom application bot
wecom.secretHint: Secret of the WeCom application bot
wecom.websocketUrlHint: Custom WebSocket server URL (leave empty to use default wss://openws.work.weixin.qq.com)
wecom.behaviorDesc: Configure message routing and trigger conditions.
wecom.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
wecom.groupTriggerHint: WeCom only delivers group messages when the bot is @mentioned, so group trigger is fixed to mention-only
wecom.allowFromHint: Only allow these user IDs to trigger Agent (leave empty to allow everyone)
wecom.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter user ID and press Enter to add
wecom.advancedDesc: Message length limit and rate control.
wecom.textChunkLimitHint: Maximum characters per message chunk, excess will be sent as separate messages (default 4000)

dingtalk.enableDesc: When enabled, the bot will connect to DingTalk via Stream long-connection and start receiving and replying to messages.
dingtalk.credentialsDesc: Fill in after creating an app in the DingTalk Open Platform with Stream mode enabled.
dingtalk.behaviorDesc: Configure message routing and user allowlist.
dingtalk.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
dingtalk.allowFromHint: Only allow these user IDs to trigger Agent (leave empty to allow everyone)
dingtalk.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter user ID and press Enter to add
dingtalk.advancedDesc: Debug mode and other advanced parameters.
dingtalk.debugHint: When enabled, detailed connection and message logs will be output

telegram.credentialsDesc: Fill in after creating a bot via @BotFather on Telegram.
telegram.modeHint: Polling: bot actively fetches messages (no public URL needed). Webhook: Telegram pushes updates to a public URL.
telegram.webhookSecretHint: Required in Webhook mode. Set this same value in Telegram's setWebhook call.
telegram.behaviorDesc: Configure message routing and trigger conditions.
telegram.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
telegram.groupTriggerHint: When to trigger Agent response in group chats
telegram.mentionContextMessagesHint: Number of recent messages to include as context when @mentioned (0 to disable)
telegram.accessControlDesc: Restrict who can interact with the bot. If allowFrom is set (even empty), pairing is required for unlisted users.
telegram.allowFromHint: Telegram user IDs allowed to use the bot. Leave key absent (remove all) for open access; set to empty list to require pairing for everyone.
telegram.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter Telegram user ID and press Enter
telegram.pairingDesc: The bot sends a one-time code to users not yet in the allowlist. Enter the code below to authorize them.
telegram.pairingSuccess: User {{userId}} paired and added to allowlist
telegram.advancedDesc: Debounce, deduplication, streaming output, and other tuning parameters.
telegram.streamingHint: When enabled, replies are edited in-place as they stream (requires bot message edit permission)
telegram.streamingCoalesceMsHint: Throttle window for streaming message edits (milliseconds)
telegram.inboundDebounceMsHint: Wait time for merging consecutive messages, 0 to disable (default 800ms)
telegram.dedupTtlSecondsHint: Retention time for message deduplication records (default 86400s = 24h)

email.credentialsDesc: Use a dedicated mailbox with IMAP enabled and an app password.
email.addressHint: Address that receives user mail and sends agent replies.
email.passwordHint: Enable IMAP/SMTP in the mailbox provider settings first, then enter the generated client authorization code or app password. Most providers do not accept the normal web login password here. Stored in the Flocks secret store.
email.serversDesc: Configure the IMAP inbox and SMTP sender used by this channel.
email.imapHostHint: Inbox server, for example imap.gmail.com or outlook.office365.com.
email.imapHostMismatchWarning: This mailbox usually uses {{expectedHost}} as the IMAP host. Check whether the host address is correct.
email.imapPortHint: Default is 993 for IMAP over SSL.
email.securityHint: Use TLS whenever possible. Enable insecure mode only when testing and you intentionally accept the risk.
email.smtpHostHint: Outgoing mail server, for example smtp.gmail.com or smtp.office365.com.
email.smtpHostMismatchWarning: This mailbox usually uses {{expectedHost}} as the SMTP host. Check whether the host address is correct.
email.smtpPortHint: Default is 587 for STARTTLS, or 465 for implicit TLS.
email.allowInsecureConnectionsLabel: I confirm insecure mode is acceptable
email.allowInsecureConnectionsHint: Only enable insecure mode in test environments or when you explicitly accept this risk.
email.accessControlDesc: Email is closed by default. Add allowed senders or explicitly allow everyone.
email.allowAllHint: Accept mail from any sender. Use only with a dedicated public inbox.
email.allowFromHint: Only these email addresses can trigger the agent.
email.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter email address and press Enter
email.requireAuthenticatedSenderHint: When an allowlist is used, require SPF/DKIM/DMARC evidence from Authentication-Results before trusting From.
email.authservIdHint: Trusted Authentication-Results authserv-id, usually your receiving mail server. Required when sender authentication is enabled.
email.behaviorDesc: Configure routing defaults for email-originated sessions and replies.
email.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified.
email.defaultSubjectHint: Subject used for proactive sends without an existing email thread.
email.pollIntervalSecondsHint: Seconds between IMAP checks. Minimum is 5 seconds.
email.skipExistingOnStartHint: Ignore all mail already in the inbox when the channel starts, including unread mail received while the channel was offline.
email.skipExistingOnStartLabel: Only process new mail after startup
email.skipAttachmentsHint: Ignore attachment metadata in inbound mail.

weixin.enableDesc: When enabled, the bot connects to WeChat personal accounts via the iLink Bot long-poll API.
weixin.credentialsDesc: Obtain Token and Account ID after scanning the iLink Bot QR code.
weixin.tokenHint: iLink Bot Token (obtained after QR login)
weixin.accountIdHint: iLink Bot Account ID (obtained after QR login, format: xxx@im.bot)
weixin.baseUrlHint: Custom iLink API base URL (leave empty to use default https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com)
weixin.behaviorDesc: Configure message routing and DM policy.
weixin.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
weixin.dmPolicyHint: Controls which users' direct messages can trigger the Agent
weixin.allowFromHint: In allowlist mode, only these WeChat user_ids can trigger the Agent
weixin.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter WeChat user_id and press Enter
weixin.groupPolicyHint: Controls whether group chat messages trigger the Agent
weixin.groupAllowFromHint: In allowlist mode, only messages from these group / room IDs trigger the Agent
weixin.groupAllowFromPlaceholder: Enter group ID and press Enter
weixin.advancedDesc: Message chunk delay and state storage directory.
weixin.sendChunkDelayHint: Delay in seconds between multi-chunk messages (default 1.5s)
weixin.dataDirHint: Directory for sync_buf and context-token state files (leave empty to use ~/.flocks/workspace/channels/weixin)
weixin.qrAlreadyLinked: Account already linked — scan again to replace credentials
weixin.qrHintScanning: Scan the QR code above with WeChat
weixin.qrHintScaned: Scanned — please tap Confirm on your phone
weixin.qrError: Failed to fetch QR code — check your network connection

whatsapp.credentialsDesc: Pair through WhatsApp Linked Devices. This channel uses a local Baileys bridge bound to 127.0.0.1 only.
whatsapp.sessionConfigured: WhatsApp is paired; scan again to replace it or bind a new account
whatsapp.qrHintScanning: Open WhatsApp Linked Devices on your phone and scan this QR code
whatsapp.qrHintComplete: Pairing complete; configuration saved and channel restarting
whatsapp.modeHint: Bot mode uses a dedicated WhatsApp account. Self-chat mode only handles messages sent to yourself.
whatsapp.behaviorDesc: Configure DM, group, and trigger policies.
whatsapp.defaultAgentHint: Default Agent ID used when no Agent is specified
whatsapp.dmPolicyHint: Controls which WhatsApp direct messages can trigger the Agent
whatsapp.allowFromEnabledHint: When enabled, DM policy uses an allowlist. When disabled, DM policy is open. Accepts phone numbers, full JIDs, or @lid IDs.
whatsapp.allowFromPlaceholder: Enter phone number or WhatsApp JID and press Enter
whatsapp.groupPolicyHint: Controls whether group chat messages can trigger the Agent
whatsapp.groupAllowFromHint: WhatsApp group IDs usually end with @g.us
whatsapp.groupAllowFromPlaceholder: Enter group JID and press Enter
whatsapp.groupTriggerHint: When to trigger Agent responses in group chats
whatsapp.advancedDesc: Local bridge port, batching, and send timeout settings.
whatsapp.bridgePortHint: Local bridge HTTP port, bound to 127.0.0.1 only (default 3100)
whatsapp.replyPrefixHint: Optional text prefix added before outgoing WhatsApp replies
whatsapp.textBatchDelaySecondsHint: Quiet period for merging rapid text messages before invoking the Agent
whatsapp.sendChunkDelayMsHint: Delay between chunks when a long reply is split
whatsapp.sendTimeoutMsHint: Per-call timeout for bridge sendMessage calls
whatsapp.mediaCacheDirHint: Directory where inbound media is downloaded (default workspace channel directory)
